title: API key printed to stdout on every API error
detail: llm_utils.py:133,136 — print(client.api_key, e) logs the full OpenAI API key to console on every error and retry. Any shared log or notebook capture exposes the key.

title: Unsafe pickle.load on checkpoint files
detail: main.py:448-452 — pickle.load(f) on checkpoint files. Malicious checkpoint from untrusted HuggingFace source enables arbitrary code execution.

title: trust_remote_code=True in model loading
detail: llm_utils.py:231,242 — AutoTokenizer and LLM both use trust_remote_code=True. Compromised HuggingFace model repo can execute arbitrary Python.
recommendation: Remove trust_remote_code=True or document trust boundary explicitly.

title: Single contributor — bus factor 1
detail: ViktorAxelsen is sole contributor with 32 commits. Academic single-author repo.

title: Zero test files in entire repository
detail: No test_*, *_test.py, conftest.py, pytest, unittest, or mock imports found across 31 Python files (12,182 lines). eval_*.sh are evaluation scripts, not tests.
recommendation: Expected for academic code. Do not depend on code correctness without independent verification.

title: Zero releases or tags
detail: No releases, no SemVer, no way to pin stable version. Commits directly to main with messages like 'sync'.
recommendation: Pin to specific commit SHA if referencing code.

title: No .gitignore file
detail: No .gitignore to exclude checkpoints/, results/, data/, *.pkl, .env. Users who fork and add data/keys will commit them.

title: 18 dependencies with zero version pinning
detail: requirements.txt has openai, transformers, langchain-community etc. all unpinned. GPU deps (torch, vllm, flash-attn) not in requirements.txt at all.

title: Minimal community health (42%)
detail: Only README and LICENSE. No CONTRIBUTING, Code of Conduct, issue templates, PR templates.

title: main.py is 42KB monolith
detail: Entry point handles dataset loading, data splitting, memory extraction, parallel inference, and ALFWorld management. Should delegate to trainer or separate orchestration module.
recommendation: Conceptual note — not our code to fix.

title: Shell scripts lack set -euo pipefail
detail: 6 shell scripts (train_*.sh, eval_*.sh) have no error trapping. Python crash mid-run doesn't halt downstream steps.
recommendation: Add error trapping for reproducible runs.

title: sys.path.append instead of proper package imports
detail: executor.py:9, designer.py:15, operation_bank.py:9, trainer.py:22 all manipulate sys.path.
recommendation: Standard academic code pattern. Not our concern.

title: POSITIVE: Well-structured src/ with single-responsibility modules
detail: controller.py (PPO), designer.py (evolution), executor.py (actions), memory_bank.py (storage), operation_bank.py (skill bank), trainer.py (orchestration). Factory pattern for datasets and evaluators. Consistent type hints and docstrings.
recommendation: Good architecture despite being academic code.

title: POSITIVE: Novel memory skills framework
detail: Meta-memory concept: skills about HOW to remember, not WHAT to remember. PPO controller for skill selection. LLM-driven 3-stage skill evolution (failure mining → analysis+reflection → refinement). Dual-embedding memory bank. 15 evolved skill files in standardized Markdown format.
recommendation: Read arXiv 2602.02474. Key extraction targets: skill bank concept, evolution loop, PPO selection mechanism.

title: POSITIVE: Comprehensive README for research repo
detail: 360 lines covering overview, setup, 4 datasets, 30 CLI flags, extension guide, citation. Better than most academic repos.
